topic.what: Preventing unauthorized fine-tuning of CodeLLMs on protected code datasets via functionality-preserving poisoning
topic.how: Injecting execution-inert weak-use code fragments into executed paths with type-aware synthesis and safety filtering
topic.why_matters: Addresses copyright/compliance concerns without breaking normal code usability unlike prior approaches that destroy compilability

characteristics.what_it_measures: Dataset fraction needed to suppress fine-tuning gains
characteristics.vs_baseline: CoProtector requires 100% full-dataset poisoning; FUNPOISON achieves comparable deterrence at 10%
characteristics.evidence: RQ1 experiments across HumanEval-X Java with 164 tasks, 984 total inputs, multiple CodeLLMs
characteristics.context: Partial poisoning under realistic attacker settings

characteristics.what_it_measures: Code remains syntactically valid and compilable after poisoning
characteristics.vs_baseline: CoProtector code corruption/splicing/renaming causes near-zero compilability
characteristics.evidence: Figure 1 left panel, Figure 3 pipeline validation
characteristics.context: Essential for preserving code usability in released datasets

characteristics.what_it_measures: Degradation in generated code quality under poisoned training
characteristics.vs_baseline: FUNPOISON poisoned models produce lower-quality code vs CoProtector's fully poisoned setup
characteristics.evidence: Figure 1 comparing CodeBLEU and Exact Match scores under full poisoning
characteristics.context: Poisoning effectiveness on model output quality

characteristics.what_it_measures: Observable program behavior preserved after injection
characteristics.vs_baseline: No side effects or behavioral changes introduced by injected fragments
characteristics.evidence: Safety filtering + execution-safe injection ensures side-effect freedom
characteristics.context: Conservative safety policy enforces behavior preservation

characteristics.vs_baseline: CoProtector filtered out by preprocessing; FUNPOISON persists
characteristics.evidence: Ablation studies on advanced code sanitization techniques in experimental setup
characteristics.context: Adaptive attacker with full pipeline control

good_for: protecting code datasets against unauthorized fine-tuning
preserving code usability while deterring misuse
not_for: protecting against authorized users with clean data access
requires: access to code corpus for template extraction

novelty: First functionality-preserving poisoning approach for code datasets
Execution-inert weak-use fragment injection maintaining 100% compilability
Template pool from real-world statement-level code with compilation repair
Conservative two-tier safety filtering for side-effect freedom
Type-aware synthesis for static-analysis evasion and stealth
open_problems: Scalability to web-scale datasets with diverse languages
Long-term adaptability against evolving sanitization techniques
Measuring deterrence effectiveness on production CodeLLMs
Generalization to other code modality tasks beyond generation
